2604.15306.title: Generalization in LLM Problem Solving: The Case of the Shortest Path
2604.15306.paper_summary: Whether language models can systematically generalize remains actively debated. Yet empirical performance is jointly shaped by multiple factors such as training data, training paradigms, and inference-time strategies, making failures difficult to interpret. We introduce a controlled synthetic environment based on shortest-path planning, a canonical composable sequential optimization problem. The setup enables clean separation of these factors and supports two orthogonal axes of generalization: spatial transfer to unseen maps and length scaling to longer-horizon problems. We find that models exhibit strong spatial transfer but consistently fail under length scaling due to recursive instability. We further analyze how distinct stages of the learning pipeline influence systematic problem-solving: for example, data coverage sets capability limits; reinforcement learning improves training stability but does not expand those limits; and inference-time scaling enhances performance but cannot rescue length-scaling failures.
2604.15306.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型是否能夠系統性地進行泛化仍然是活躍的辯論主題。<lb/>然而，實證表現是由多個因素共同影響的，例如訓練數據、訓練範式和推理時間策略，這使得失敗的解釋變得困難。<lb/>我們介紹了一個基於最短路徑規劃的受控合成環境，這是一個典型的可組合序列優化問題。<lb/>該設置能夠清晰地分離這些因素，並支持兩個正交的泛化軸：對未見地圖的空間轉移和對更長時間範圍問題的長度縮放。<lb/>我們發現模型在空間轉移方面表現強勁，但在長度縮放下卻始終失敗，原因是遞歸不穩定性。<lb/>我們進一步分析了學習流程的不同階段如何影響系統性問題解決：例如，數據覆蓋設置了能力限制；強化學習提高了訓練穩定性，但並未擴展這些限制；而推理時間的縮放提升了性能，但無法挽救長度縮放的失敗。
2604.15306.author: Yao Tong et.al.
2604.15306.authors: Yao Tong, Jiayuan Ye, Anastasia Borovykh, Reza Shokri
